test.name: Database Error One

kw.name: Raise Value Error
kw.msg: ValueError: database connection failed: unable to connect to server at host.example.com:5432, connection timeout after 30 seconds, please check your database credentials and network connectivity

Traceback (most recent call last): File "/Users/tatuaalto/workspace/robotframework-analysis/tests/fixtures/test_library.py", line 6, in raise_value_error raise ValueError( ValueError: database connection failed: unable to connect to server at host.example.com:5432, connection timeout after 30 seconds, please check your database credentials and network connectivity
kw.doc: Raise a ValueError with a realistic message.
kw.status: ValueError: database connection failed: unable to connect to server at host.example.com:5432, connection timeout after 30 seconds, please check your database credentials and network connectivity

test.name: Login Timeout

kw.name: Raise Type Error
kw.msg: TypeError: TypeError: expected argument of type str or bytes-like object, not NoneType. Function signature requires (name: str, age: int, email: str, phone: str, address: str, city: str, state: str, zip_code: str, country: str, company: str, job_title: str, department: str, manager: str, salary: int, hire_date: str, termination_date: str, is_active: bool, is_admin: bool) but received NoneType for parameter 'name'. This is typically caused by passing None where a string is expected. To fix this, ensure all required string parameters are properly initialized before calling this function. You may want to check your data source, API response, or configuration file to ensure values are not null. The full traceback shows the call originated from line 42 in process_user_data() which was called from setup_database() at line 18.

Traceback (most recent call last): File "/Users/tatuaalto/workspace/robotframework-analysis/tests/fixtures/test_library.py", line 30, in raise_type_error raise TypeError(long_message) TypeError: TypeError: expected argument of type str or bytes-like object, not NoneType. Function signature requires (name: str, age: int, email: str, phone: str, address: str, city: str, state: str, zip_code: str, country: str, company: str, job_title: str, department: str, manager: str, salary: int, hire_date: str, termination_date: str, is_active: bool, is_admin: bool) but received NoneType for parameter 'name'. This is typically caused by passing None where a string is expected. To fix this, ensure all required string parameters are properly initialized before calling this function. You may want to check your data source, API response, or configuration file to ensure values are not null. The full traceback shows the call originated from line 42 in process_user_data() which was called from setup_database() at line 18.
kw.doc: Raise a TypeError with a very long message.
kw.status: TypeError: TypeError: expected argument of type str or bytes-like object, not NoneType. Function signature requires (name: str, age: int, email: str, phone: str, address: str, city: str, state: str, zip_code: str, country: str, company: str, job_title: str, department: str, manager: str, salary: int, hire_date: str, termination_date: str, is_active: bool, is_admin: bool) but received NoneType for parameter 'name'. This is typically caused by passing None where a string is expected. To fix this, ensure all required string parameters are properly initialized before calling this function. You may want to check your data source, API response, or configuration file to ensure values are not null. The full traceback shows the call originated from line 42 in process_user_data() which was called from setup_database() at line 18.

test.status: TypeError: TypeError: expected argument of type str or bytes-like object, not NoneType. Function signature requires (name: str, age: int, email: str, phone: str, address: str, city: str, state: str, zip_code: str, country: str, company: str, job_title: str, department: str, manager: str, salary: int, hire_date: str, termination_date: str, is_active: bool, is_admin: bool) but received NoneType for parameter 'name'. This is typically caused by passing None where a string is expected. To fix this, ensure all required string parameters are properly initialized before calling this function. You may want to check your data source, API response, or configuration file to ensure values are not null. The full traceback shows the call originated from line 42 in process_user_data() which was called from setup_database() at line 18.
